pages.text: A chaveta is the tool used to cut processed tobacco into usable forms. Once tobacco has been cured, and optionally fermented or aged, it can be cut for different finished products.
The position of the chaveta in a crafting recipe can determine the resulting cut. This lets one tool create multiple tobacco textures and product types without adding unnecessary item clutter.

Typical cuts include ribbon cut, rough cut, flake cut, and fine cut. Different cuts may be used in pipe blends, cigarettes, cigars, or hookah preparations. A coarse cut may burn slower, while a finer cut may pack more easily or mix more smoothly.
A good cutting system should make processed tobacco feel versatile. The same leaf can become very different products depending on how it is cut, blended, and prepared.
